mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, lifting the present value of S&P 500 earnings and driving positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden deterioration in labor demand that triggers a recessionary repricing of equities, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or trigger frequency exceeds 15% without corresponding volatility expansion.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of residential housing starts and real consumer credit indicates a synchronized withdrawal of household balance sheet support, which reduces aggregate demand and forces a downward revision of corporate profit forecasts, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not differ significantly between joint contraction and joint expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion characterized by tight labor markets, a sustained decline in the industrial production index reflects supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints that compress corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO declines alongside UNRATE below 4.0%, contradicting the margin compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand that directly lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-trading-day window.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in quarters with accelerating GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently high to trigger a valuation multiple compression in growth-oriented equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of payroll employment and a rise in the unemployment rate indicates a labor market paradox where job creation outpaces labor force participation, signaling structural demand weakness that eventually drags down equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE rises concurrently, compared to periods of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of persistent inflation above 3.5%, a decline in real consumer credit reflects monetary policy tightening that successfully cools demand, but the resulting disinflationary shock initially depresses equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and RSAFS declines, contradicting the risk premium expansion thesis.
